cards.title: 2028 Republican presidential nominee
cards.headline: J.D. Vance Is Underpriced for the 2028 GOP Nomination
cards.summary: Vance's 37.5% market price significantly underestimates the historical dominance of sitting Vice Presidents in primaries, his commanding 42% polling lead, and his lock on party infrastructure. The best opportunities are buying Vance and selling overpriced longshots like Tucker Carlson and Donald Trump Sr.
why: Vice Presidents who seek their party's nomination have historically won ~90% of the time, and Vance holds a 28-point polling lead over his nearest rival
Tucker Carlson at 7.65% is wildly overpriced after publicly breaking with Trump and lacking any campaign infrastructure
Donald Trump Sr. at 3.55% is essentially free money to sell — the 22nd Amendment bars him from a third term
cards.watch_item: A significant escalation of U.S. military involvement in Iran could crater Vance's approval and open the door to a serious challenger like Rubio or DeSantis

cards.title: Which world leaders will leave office in 2026?
cards.headline: Cuba's Díaz-Canel Wildly Overpriced to Leave Office in 2026
cards.summary: The market prices Díaz-Canel at a 58.5% chance of leaving office this year, but with no democratic mechanism to remove him and his explicit refusal to step down, the fair probability is closer to 18% — a massive mispricing.
why: Díaz-Canel has publicly stated he will not resign, and Cuba's single-party system has no pathway for forced removal
Cuba's authoritarian precedent is clear: Fidel Castro held power for 49 years without being ousted
No credible internal opposition, military threat, or external regime-change effort is currently in play
cards.watch_item: A sudden health crisis, internal Communist Party power struggle, or dramatic escalation of U.S.-Cuba tensions could shift the calculus

cards.title: When will Trump visit China?
cards.headline: Trump's China Visit Confirmed for May 14–15 — Market Severely Underpricing 'Before May 15'
cards.summary: Both the White House and China's foreign ministry have officially confirmed a state visit for May 14–15, 2026, with active diplomatic preparations underway. The 'Before May 15' contract at 53% dramatically underprices what is now a confirmed, imminent trip just days away.
why: State visit officially confirmed by both governments for May 14–15, with high-level prep calls already happening
The Iran ceasefire that caused the original March postponement is holding, removing the key prior disruption risk
Market at 53% implies a coin flip for an event that is actively scheduled and 13 days out — roughly 29 points of edge
cards.watch_item: Any sudden geopolitical crisis — such as an Iran flare-up, Taiwan escalation, or diplomatic breakdown over trade terms — that could force a second postponement

cards.title: Will a humanoid robot walk on Mars before a human does?
cards.headline: Humanoid Robot on Mars by 2035? Market Overprices a Near-Impossible Chain of Firsts
cards.summary: The market prices a humanoid robot walking on Mars before 2035 at ~47%, but the unprecedented chain of technological achievements required — an untested interplanetary Starship, a space-hardened humanoid robot, and successful Mars landing and deployment — makes fair value closer to 22%.
why: No humanoid robot has ever operated on any extraterrestrial surface, and space-hardening one for Mars conditions requires years beyond current Earth prototypes
Starship hasn't completed orbital refueling or any Mars-capable mission, with only 3 realistic Mars transfer windows left (2028-29, 2031, 2033)
Historical Mars landing success rates hover around 50%, and ambitious space timelines consistently slip 3-5x from initial promises
cards.watch_item: SpaceX achieving successful orbital refueling and a Mars-bound Starship demonstration flight by 2028 would significantly shift the odds upward

cards.title: Who will Trump pardon?
cards.headline: Pete Rose Can't Be Pardoned — He's Dead
cards.summary: The market prices a 23.5% chance Trump pardons Pete Rose, but Rose died on September 30, 2024, making a presidential pardon legally impossible. This represents the clearest mispricing across all 49 pardon contracts.
why: Pete Rose passed away in September 2024 — you cannot pardon a deceased person
The market still prices this at 23.5% despite a near-zero fair probability
This offers roughly 21.5 percentage points of edge with high confidence
cards.watch_item: Any unprecedented symbolic or posthumous gesture by Trump that the market's resolution criteria might count as a 'pardon' — extremely unlikely but worth monitoring

cards.title: Will Elon win his case against OpenAI?
cards.headline: Musk v. OpenAI: Market Overprices a Legally Uphill Battle
cards.summary: With Musk having dropped 24 of 26 claims before trial and facing a major donor-standing hurdle, the market at 57% significantly overestimates his chances of winning — fair probability sits closer to 36%.
why: Donors historically lack legal standing to enforce charitable trusts — that right belongs to the state AG, creating a fundamental barrier with almost no favorable precedent for Musk
Dropping 24 of 26 claims (including all fraud charges) before trial signals pre-trial weakness, leaving only narrow equitable claims
Structural asymmetry favors NO: hung jury, mistrial, standing dismissal, or unresolved appeal all count as a Musk loss under market rules
cards.watch_item: Whether the judge creates novel precedent on donor standing — if she allows Musk's claims to proceed on the merits despite the doctrine, the legal calculus shifts dramatically in his favor.

cards.title: Will Trump be impeached and removed from office?
cards.headline: Trump Removal Market Vastly Overpriced at 26.5%
cards.summary: No U.S. president has ever been removed via impeachment, and the structural barriers — especially the 67-vote Senate threshold — make this outcome extremely unlikely even over a 2.7-year horizon. Fair probability sits around 6%, creating a substantial edge for NO.
why: Zero presidents removed by impeachment in 235 years of U.S. history (0 for 46)
Senate conviction requires ~17 Republican defections — far beyond the 7 who crossed over in 2021
Democrats are internally divided on impeachment and may not even control the House until 2027
cards.watch_item: A major, unprecedented crisis — such as a nuclear incident or massive scandal — that fractures the Republican Senate coalition beyond anything seen in modern politics

cards.title: Where will Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding occur?
cards.headline: Rhode Island Wedding Venue Rumor Debunked — Market Still Pricing It at 25%
cards.summary: Multiple credible reports confirm save-the-dates have been sent for a July 3, 2026 New York City wedding, while the rumored Rhode Island venue (Ocean House) has been explicitly denied by the venue itself and its wedding planner. Rhode Island at 25.5% is dramatically overpriced.
why: Save-the-dates for a July 3, 2026 NYC wedding have already been distributed to guests
Ocean House in Rhode Island publicly denied any booking, debunking the main alternative venue rumor
With the wedding roughly two months away and invitations out, a last-minute venue switch to RI is extremely unlikely
cards.watch_item: Any credible report of a venue change or wedding postponement — though with save-the-dates already sent, this would be highly unusual

cards.title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by April 22, 2026?
cards.headline: US-Iran Permanent Peace Deal by April 22 Is Near-Impossible
cards.summary: With fewer than 24 hours until the deadline, talks haven't even begun, Iran hasn't confirmed attendance, and the resolution requires a formal permanent peace deal—not a framework, ceasefire, or memorandum of understanding. The market at ~19.5% dramatically overprices YES.
why: Resolution demands a PERMANENT deal—an impossibly high bar when prior rounds collapsed and parties remain 'worlds apart' on nuclear issues
Iran hasn't confirmed participation in Islamabad talks; its IRGC is pressuring negotiators to refuse under the current blockade
Trump's repeated claims of imminent deals have already proven unreliable, and current draft proposals focus on explicitly temporary measures
cards.watch_item: A surprise joint announcement or ambiguous declaration by both governments that could be interpreted as meeting the 'permanent deal' resolution criteria

cards.title: How high will inflation get this year?
cards.headline: Inflation Hitting 4.5%+ This Year Is Overpriced at 60.5¢
cards.summary: Despite elevated oil prices from the Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruption, most forecasters expect peak U.S. inflation to cluster around 4.0–4.2% — well below the 4.5% threshold the market is pricing at a 60.5% probability. The fair probability is closer to 48%, offering meaningful edge on the sell side.
why: March CPI came in at 3.3% YoY with core inflation a tame 2.6%, suggesting limited second-round price pressures
Consensus peak inflation forecasts from major institutions cluster around 4.0–4.2%, not 4.5%+
The Fed is holding rates at 3.5–3.75%, actively constraining demand-side inflation and capping upside
cards.watch_item: A major escalation in the Iran conflict pushing oil above $130/barrel could drive headline CPI past 4.5% and invalidate the sell thesis

cards.title: 2028 Democratic presidential nominee
cards.headline: Kamala Harris Is Dramatically Underpriced for 2028 Democratic Nomination
cards.summary: Harris polls at 22-24% in early Democratic primary surveys but trades at just 6.4% on Kalshi — the largest polling-to-price gap in the market. Even after discounting for the possibility she doesn't run, fair value sits around 14%, more than double the current price.
why: Harris leads national Democratic primary polls at 22-24% while trading at only 6.4% — a massive disconnect
She is actively signaling a 2028 run with policy speeches and party positioning, plus retains unmatched name ID and donor infrastructure
Other candidates like Ossoff (8.6% with zero polling support) appear overpriced, suggesting broad market mispricing
cards.watch_item: Whether Harris makes a definitive decision not to run — that would collapse her value instantly and is the single biggest risk to this bet

cards.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of May?
cards.headline: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Unlikely to Normalize by End of May
cards.summary: With daily ship transits running at roughly 6–20 vessels versus the ~60 needed to meet the market's threshold, and an active US-Iran military confrontation showing no signs of resolution, a return to normal traffic by May 31 faces overwhelming obstacles.
why: Current traffic is 8–9x below the required threshold, with only 30 days left and no historical precedent for such rapid recovery from an active blockade
War-risk insurance premiums have surged from 0.25% to 5% of hull value, creating a structural drag that would persist even after a hypothetical ceasefire
No deal framework exists between the US and Iran, and expert forecasts (including major energy and economic analysts) project no full reopening until August at the earliest
cards.watch_item: A sudden comprehensive US-Iran diplomatic breakthrough — Iran has submitted new negotiation proposals and previously briefly reopened the strait, showing that rapid shifts remain possible even if unlikely

cards.title: Who will be the next Supreme Court justice?
cards.headline: Ron DeSantis at 10.5% for Next SCOTUS Justice Is a Clear Overprice
cards.summary: With no Supreme Court vacancy in sight and DeSantis lacking judicial experience or credible shortlist placement, the market is pricing him far above his realistic ~3% chance of becoming the next justice.
why: No SCOTUS vacancy exists — both Alito and Thomas have denied 2026 retirement plans
DeSantis has zero judicial experience and appears on no credible insider shortlists, while proven judges like Ho and Oldham dominate frontrunner reporting
The market is oversubscribed at ~113% total, systematically inflating speculative long-shot names
cards.watch_item: Any credible reporting of an Alito or Thomas retirement, or Trump publicly floating DeSantis as a potential nominee, would dramatically reshape this market

cards.title: 2026 2026 Midterms: Congress Balance of Power?
cards.headline: Democrats Favored to Sweep Congress in 2026 — Market Underpricing the Blue Wave
cards.summary: A D+10 generic ballot lead and record GOP House retirements make Democratic House control near-certain (~85%), while Senate control is a genuine coin flip (~51-52% Dem), creating a combined ~47% chance of a full Democratic sweep — slightly above the market's 44.5%.
why: Generic ballot polling shows Democrats leading by ~10 points, historically consistent with a strong House wave
A record 37 Republican House retirements are opening up vulnerable seats across the map
Republican full-control scenario (R-House, R-Senate) is the clearest mispricing at 18.5% vs. a fair value near 12%
cards.watch_item: Toss-up Senate races in the next six months — an economic shock or major scandal could swing the roughly 50/50 Senate picture decisively in either direction

cards.title: Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?
cards.headline: Anthropic Has the IPO Momentum, but Neither Side Offers a Strong Bet
cards.summary: Anthropic is actively eyeing an October 2026 IPO with a $1T valuation and clean governance, while OpenAI faces revenue misses and CFO reservations about timing — but a 14-year resolution window makes current signals unreliable enough to warrant staying on the sidelines.
why: Anthropic is reportedly weighing an October 2026 IPO and rejected an $800B pre-IPO round, signaling readiness to go public first
OpenAI missed revenue and user targets, with analysts saying it likely won't go public this year despite massive capital needs
Both contracts are close to fair value after accounting for the ~7% market vig, leaving no clear edge on either side
cards.watch_item: Any announcement that OpenAI has formally filed or accelerated IPO plans due to its $600B spending commitments would dramatically shift the calculus

cards.title: When will any company achieve AGI? · Before Jan 1, 2027
cards.headline: Formal AGI Declaration Before 2027 Remains Unlikely — Market Overpriced
cards.summary: No company has made or signaled intent to make an official AGI announcement, and strong disincentives make one unlikely before January 1, 2027. The market at 14.5% overprices this outcome relative to a fair estimate near 9%.
why: Resolution requires an explicit official corporate announcement using the term 'AGI' — a bar no company has ever met
Major labs face regulatory, liability, and reputational risks from declaring AGI, creating strong disincentives
Industry trend is shifting toward practical AI applications, not moonshot AGI claims — even OpenAI removed its AGI clause from its Microsoft deal
cards.watch_item: Any formal press release or official corporate statement from a frontier AI lab explicitly claiming AGI achievement — particularly from unpredictable actors like Elon Musk's xAI

cards.title: Who will be Trump's next Secretary of Labor?
cards.headline: Glenn Youngkin Overpriced for Labor Secretary at 8%
cards.summary: With the Labor Secretary seat vacant since late April 2026 and acting Secretary Keith Sonderling holding the post, the market is overvaluing Glenn Youngkin's chances at 8% — reporting indicates White House frustration with Youngkin, and he has no labor policy background.
why: Reports indicate White House friction with Youngkin over Virginia redistricting disputes
Youngkin has no meaningful labor policy background or credible links to the role
Acting Secretary Sonderling holds the incumbent advantage and business community support
cards.watch_item: Any signal that Trump is formally considering Youngkin for a Cabinet role despite reported tensions would invalidate the thesis

cards.title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 15, 2026?
cards.headline: US-Iran Permanent Peace Deal by May 15 Is Nearly Impossible
cards.summary: With roughly 13 days remaining and no face-to-face talks scheduled, a formal permanent peace deal between the US and Iran is virtually out of reach. The market at 8.5% significantly overprices the chance of resolution.
why: Core disputes on nuclear enrichment and sanctions sequencing remain unresolved, with the US rejecting Iran's latest proposal
No direct negotiations are scheduled—proposals are still being shuttled through Pakistani intermediaries, and US envoy travel was canceled in late April
The resolution bar is extraordinarily high: a formal 'permanent' peace deal confirmed by both governments, not a framework or ceasefire extension
cards.watch_item: Any surprise announcement of direct US-Iran talks or a Trump-driven dramatic diplomatic pivot could rapidly shift odds

cards.title: Will Blue Origin land on the moon before SpaceX?
cards.headline: Blue Origin Moon Landing Odds Look Slightly Overpriced
cards.summary: The market prices Blue Origin landing on the moon before SpaceX at 64.5%, but a fair estimate is closer to 60%. A modest NO position captures edge from both Blue Origin's execution struggles and the meaningful chance neither company lands before the 2030 deadline.
why: Blue Origin's simpler single-launch architecture is real, but New Glenn remains grounded after repeated upper-stage failures — execution risk is high
There's a 15-25% chance neither company lands on the moon before 2030, and that entire scenario resolves NO
SpaceX's orbital refueling challenge (10-15 tanker flights) is daunting, but its iteration pace and launch cadence partially offset that complexity
cards.watch_item: New Glenn's return to flight — a quick and successful resumption would shift odds meaningfully toward Blue Origin and erode the NO edge

cards.title: Who will leave Trump's Cabinet next?
cards.headline: Tulsi Gabbard Is the Most Likely Next Cabinet Departure
cards.summary: Gabbard is the clear frontrunner to be the next Trump Cabinet member out, with a fair probability around 60% versus the market's 55.5%, offering a modest but real edge for buyers.
why: Gabbard has been visibly sidelined — reports of internal friction over FISA 702 and growing distrust from Trump's inner circle
Three women have already departed the Cabinet (Noem, Bondi, Chavez-DeRemer), and Gabbard fits the emerging pattern
The market's total probabilities sum to ~162%, systematically overpricing long-shot candidates and compressing the frontrunner's edge
cards.watch_item: If Kash Patel — reportedly on thin ice but not listed in this market — departs first, it would delay resolution and tie up capital without paying out

cards.title: 2028 U.S. Presidential Election winner?
cards.headline: Marco Rubio Overpriced at 15.5% for 2028 Presidential Win
cards.summary: Rubio's 15.5% market price significantly overstates his realistic path to the presidency, with actual primary polls showing him at just 14% compared to J.D. Vance's commanding 42% among GOP voters. Selling Rubio offers roughly 4.5% edge based on the compound probability of winning both a contested primary and the general election.
why: Rubio trails Vance badly in real primary polls (14% vs 42%), despite a misleading CPAC straw poll surge that historically has little predictive value
J.D. Vance holds massive structural advantages as sitting Vice President, making Rubio's path to the GOP nomination steep and narrow
A fair compound probability of ~11% for Rubio to win both the nomination and the general election leaves meaningful downside in the current 15.5% price
cards.watch_item: Vance's historically low approval ratings (37%) — if they collapse further or Iran-related policy backlash lands squarely on the VP, Rubio could emerge as the credible GOP alternative and close the polling gap

cards.title: Will the SAVE Act become law?
cards.headline: SAVE Act Faces Near-Impossible Path After Senate Defeat
cards.summary: The SAVE Act passed the House but was voted down 48-50 in the Senate, with four Republican senators joining all Democrats in opposition. With the 60-vote filibuster threshold still in place and no viable path to the supermajority needed, the bill has very low odds of becoming law.
why: Already failed a Senate floor vote 48-50, and bills that fail at this stage historically have less than a 5% chance of revival
The 60-vote filibuster threshold remains intact — Senate leadership continues to resist the nuclear option despite White House pressure
Zero Democratic support and bipartisan Republican defections make assembling even a simple majority unlikely, let alone a supermajority
cards.watch_item: Any serious movement toward a filibuster carve-out or attaching SAVE Act provisions as a rider to must-pass legislation like a government funding bill

cards.title: 2027 Pro Football Champion
cards.headline: Baltimore Ravens Underpriced for Super Bowl LXII
cards.summary: At 5.5% on Kalshi, Baltimore is meaningfully cheaper than the ~7–9% implied by major sportsbooks, offering roughly 2 percentage points of edge on an elite quarterback-led contender.
why: Sportsbooks consistently price Baltimore around 7–9% implied probability — well above Kalshi's 5.5%
Lamar Jackson remains one of the NFL's elite quarterbacks with a roster built to contend
NFL parity and a wide-open conference create value on underpriced contenders this far from kickoff
cards.watch_item: Key Baltimore injuries, roster moves, or offseason trades that could shift their competitive outlook before the season begins

cards.title: Who will be confirmed as Fed chair?
cards.headline: Kevin Warsh Remains Overwhelming Favorite for Fed Chair — But Market Leaves No Room to Trade
cards.summary: Warsh is the only formal nominee, has completed his confirmation hearing, and has roughly 2.7 years of runway to clear the Senate. At 95¢, the market is pricing him slightly rich versus a ~92% fair probability, but the thin edge and long capital lockup make trading unattractive.
why: Sen. Tillis is blocking the committee vote until a DOJ probe of Powell is dropped, but the hold is tactical — Tillis says he 'likes Warsh' and is retiring in January
No alternative candidate is formally nominated, and all three listed alternatives (Shelton, Rieder, Hassett) trade at trivial penny-level prices with negligible edge
With the market closing January 1, 2029, the extended timeline heavily favors eventual confirmation even if the current standoff drags on
cards.watch_item: Whether Trump agrees to drop the DOJ investigation into former Chair Powell — resolving this would likely clear Tillis's blockade and push Warsh's confirmation probability toward near-certainty
